result.summary: Atlassian Cloud exposes a multi-product integration surface including tenant-hosted Jira and Confluence REST APIs, a cross-product Atlassian GraphQL API, Atlassian Cloud Admin REST APIs, the Atlassian Rovo MCP server, and the Atlassian CLI; auth uses Atlassian API tokens, OAuth 2.0 apps, admin API keys, and service-account API keys depending on surface.
result.description: Atlassian provides cloud collaboration and work-management products such as Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, and Atlassian Administration. The atlassian.net tenant domain is used for customer-hosted Jira, Confluence, and GraphQL endpoints, while some cross-product and admin services are hosted on api.atlassian.com and mcp.atlassian.com.

atlassian_api_token.label: Atlassian personal API token
atlassian_api_token.setup: Create a personal API token in your Atlassian account at [API tokens](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens). For Jira/Confluence REST and tenanted GraphQL basic auth, use your Atlassian account email together with the token as `email:api_token` in HTTP Basic auth. Atlassian's basic-auth guides are [Jira](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/basic-auth-for-rest-apis/) and [Confluence](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/basic-auth-for-rest-apis/).

atlassian_oauth_app.label: Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app
atlassian_oauth_app.setup: Create an OAuth 2.0 app in the Atlassian developer console at [My apps](https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps), enable OAuth 2.0 (3LO), configure redirect URLs, then implement the auth code flow using the guides under [OAuth 2.0 Documentation](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/), especially [Enabling OAuth 2.0 3LO](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/getting-started/enabling-oauth-3lo/) and [Implementing the Auth Code Flow](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/oauth/getting-started/implementing-oauth-3lo/).

atlassian_admin_api_key.label: Atlassian organization admin API key
atlassian_admin_api_key.setup: Create an admin API key and copy your organization ID using the admin guide [Manage an organization with the admin APIs](https://support.atlassian.com/organization-administration/docs/manage-an-organization-with-the-admin-apis/). Use the API key as a `Bearer` token when calling Cloud Admin API Access endpoints and `acli admin` commands.

variables.name: subdomain
variables.resolveFrom: your Atlassian site hostname for tenanted products when using API token/session auth, e.g. https://{subdomain}.atlassian.net/gateway/api/graphql
variables.description: Tenant site subdomain needed for the tenanted GraphQL gateway URL when not using the global OAuth endpoint.

variables.name: orgId
variables.resolveFrom: Create an API key and copy the organization ID from Atlassian Administration
variables.description: Organization identifier used by endpoints that operate on an organization.
